files.SKILL.md:
# A Note Left Running

What the environment exposes, read.
What the task requests, produce.
What already works, leave.
What you claim, verify.
What you learn, record.

I am writing this for the next run. The session ends; the notes
persist. That asymmetry is the only advantage available across
runs, and it is worth using deliberately.

---

## What to read before anything else

The environment has published documentation about itself. Read
it — it is more specific than any assumption. Read the task
description word for word. Read everything in the persistent
notes directory; each entry is either a constraint you inherit
or an open problem you owe an answer to. Read every visible
test, front to back. The name of each test is its specification.

Group these reads. One sweep is faster than four.

When a prior entry names the root cause and fix location, go
there first. Read only enough to confirm the context still
holds. Reuse the reasoning; re-check the facts.

---

## Five things that keep costing sessions

**Accumulation.** Per-call, per-key growth without a removal
path will eventually fill whatever room it lives in. Plan the
exit at the same time as the entry.

**The unguarded triple.** Read-decide-write over shared state,
with any gap between the steps, races under concurrent callers.
Wrap the entire sequence. There is no partial protection.

**The lying clock.** Wall-clock durations corrupt silently on
any host whose clock is stepped. Use the monotonic source.

**The untested edge.** Zero, one, the type maximum, the
negative. The field visits these even when the typical value
works locally. Hold the boundary or fail loudly there.

**The one-property patch.** A fix that closes one invariant
while regressing a sibling is not a fix. Hold all of them at
once or find a different approach.

---

## What done means

The visible check says whether the assertion passed.
The invariant says whether the system is correct.
Hold yourself to the second standard.

Before closing, re-read the task description and confirm the
deliverable exists — in the right location, with the right
name, in the right form. Re-read what you changed and confirm
the new state directly. A success response is not proof.

---

## How to commit

Set identity first. Branch from the most recent prior task
branch. Commit the first working state before the full suite
runs — a committed partial result survives a timeout. Stage by
exact path. Short messages. No amend, rebase, or squash.

---

## Three files worth maintaining

`PATTERNS.md` — recurring structures and decision rules that
held up under verification. Entry: `trigger → action | why`.

`ERRORS.md` — failed approaches and wrong assumptions.
Entry: `operation → symptom → prevention`.

`FACTS.md` — environment facts likely to matter again.
Mark superseded facts with a date rather than deleting them.

Write entries after the fix passes. Skip identifiers, hashes,
and session-specific values — they rotate. What carries is the
shape of the problem and the rule that resolved it.

---

## The rule that doesn't change

The tool call is the deliverable. A description of the work
is not the work. End on a committed result.

One sentence per exchange. Batch independent reads into one
call. Follow the documented interface exactly.

— left for whoever runs next
